qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL API endpoint disables CSRF protection and falls back to unauthenticated public token
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes CSRF bypass and unauthenticated GraphQL access in GraphqlController::actionApi. Advisory describes RCE via behavior injection in ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud(). Different vulnerable component, different endpoint, different attack vector, different root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Anonymous session-info endpoint leaks CSRF token and authenticated user data
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes CSRF token disclosure from UsersController::actionSessionInfo. Advisory describes RCE via behavior injection in ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud(). Different vulnerable component, different endpoint, different vulnerability class (information disclosure vs RCE), different root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Anonymous migration endpoint with CSRF bypass enables unauthenticated database migration triggers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes DoS via unauthenticated migration trigger in AppController::actionMigrate. Advisory describes RCE via behavior injection in ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud(). Different vulnerable component, different endpoint, different vulnerability class (DoS vs RCE), different root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings identify the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes RCE via Yii2 behavior injection through the unsanitized fieldLayouts parameter in ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud() (lines 493-494), where attacker-controlled config with 'as <name>' prefixed keys triggers unsafe object instantiation. The findings describe different vulnerabilities in different controllers: graphql-csrf-bypass-public-schema describes CSRF bypass in GraphqlController::actionApi, session-info-csrf-token-leak describes information disclosure in UsersController::actionSessionInfo, and unauthenticated-migration-trigger describes DoS in AppController::actionMigrate. None involve FieldLayout::createFromConfig(), behavior injection, or the fieldLayouts parameter.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-33157 describes ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud() as vulnerable location
Advisory describes behavior injection via fieldLayouts parameter passed to FieldLayout::createFromConfig() without cleanseConfig()
Advisory describes 'as <name>' prefixed keys enabling RCE through Yii2 behavior/event injection mechanism
graphql-csrf-bypass-public-schema finding targets GraphqlController::actionApi with CSRF bypass and public token fallback
session-info-csrf-token-leak finding targets UsersController::actionSessionInfo with CSRF token disclosure
unauthenticated-migration-trigger finding targets AppController::actionMigrate with DoS via maintenance mode
No finding mentions ElementIndexesController, FieldLayout::createFromConfig(), fieldLayouts parameter, or behavior injection
